items.front: Prerequisite for providing radar service (5-3-1)
items.back: Establish and maintain radar identification of the aircraft involved, except as provided in 5-5-1b2, b3 and 8-5-5.

items.front: Establish and maintain radar identification of the aircraft involved before providing radar service.
items.back: 5-3-1. Exceptions: 5-5-1 subparagraphs b2, b3 and 8-5-5 Radar Identification Application.

items.front: What are the three paragraph references cited in 5-3-1?
items.back: Para 3-1-9 (Use of Tower Radar Displays), Para 5-1-1 (Presentation and Equipment Performance).

items.front: Primary radar identification method: departing aircraft
items.back: Target within 1 mile of takeoff runway end at a towered airport, with verbal or nonverbal rolling/boundary notification accomplished.

items.front: Two coordination methods for departure observation ID (5-3-2a)
items.back: 1. Verbal rolling/boundary notification. 2. Nonverbal rolling/boundary notification (manual/electronic drop tube or automation).

items.front: Position correlation ID method requirements (5-3-2b)
items.back: Target position matches a fix/visual reporting point that corresponds with a direct position report, AND observed track is consistent with reported heading or route of flight.

items.front: TACAN/VORTAC fix exception under 5-3-2b
items.back: If a TACAN/VORTAC is located within 6,000 feet of the radar antenna, it may be used as a reference fix without being displayed on the video map or map overlay.

items.front: Military TACAN DME complication (5-3-2b note)
items.back: Military TACANs not collocated with frequency-paired VORs may be separated by up to 31 miles, complicating DME-based radar identification.

items.front: Identifying turn requirements under 5-3-2c
items.back: 30 degrees or more. Conditions: (1) pilot position report assures aircraft is within radar coverage/displayed area (except lost aircraft); (2) only ONE aircraft observed making turns; (3) IFR: heading away from increased-minima area or climb to highest MIA in area first.

items.front: Three conditions for identifying turns under 5-3-2c
items.back: 1. Pilot position report confirming aircraft within radar coverage (except lost aircraft). 2. Only one aircraft observed making turns. 3. For IFR: issue heading away from area requiring increased minimum IFR altitude or climb to highest MIA in area first.

items.front: IFR altitude protection before identifying turn heading (5-3-2c3)
items.back: Either issue a heading AWAY from an area requiring increased minimum IFR altitude, OR have aircraft climb to the HIGHEST minimum altitude in your area of jurisdiction before issuing any heading.

items.front: Four beacon/ADS-B identification methods (5-3-3)
items.back: a. IDENT feature activation and observation. b. Code change and observation. c. Standby disappearance then normal reappearance. d. EN ROUTE: full data block auto-associated with target on computer-assigned discrete code.

items.front: IDENT phraseology (5-3-3a)
items.back: IDENT. Or: SQUAWK (code) AND IDENT.

items.front: Standby identification method phraseology (5-3-3c)
items.back: SQUAWK STANDBY. Then after sufficient scans confirming disappearance: SQUAWK NORMAL.

items.front: Code assignment phraseology when altitude reporting is off (5-3-3d)
items.back: SQUAWK (4 digit discrete code), SQUAWK ALTITUDE.

items.front: ERAM paired LDB note (5-3-3d note)
items.back: Paired LDBs in ERAM do not display a beacon code.

items.front: En route automated identification via data block (5-3-3d)
items.back: Aircraft considered identified when full data block is automatically associated with target symbol of aircraft squawking a discrete code assigned by the computer.

items.front: Terminal auto-acquired identification conditions (5-3-4a)
items.back: Data block displayed and visible to controller, AND (1) radar/beacon ID procedures confirmed tagged target, OR (2) NAS automated handoff with NONE of these indicators: CST, NAT, NT, AMB, OLD, AM, TRK.

items.front: Seven data block indicators that prevent automated-handoff ID (5-3-4a2)
items.back: CST, NAT, NT, AMB, OLD, AM, TRK.

items.front: When is data block NOT used to maintain target identity? (5-3-4b)
items.back: When it is in a coast status or displaced from the appropriate target.

items.front: Displaced data block requirement (5-3-4c)
items.back: A displaced data block must be updated at all times.

items.front: When to use multiple identification methods (5-3-5a)
items.back: When proximity of targets, duplication of observed action, or any other circumstances cause doubt as to target identification.

items.front: Action when identification is questionable (5-3-5b)
items.back: Take immediate action to re-identify the aircraft OR terminate radar service. Use 5-3-2 (primary) or 5-3-3 (beacon/ADS-B) methods.

items.front: En route additional requirement when ID lost/questionable (5-3-5b2)
items.back: Ensure that all primary targets are displayed.

items.front: When position information must be given (5-3-6)
items.back: Whenever radar identification is established by identifying turns or by any beacon identification method outlined in 5-3-3.

items.front: When position information is NOT required (5-3-6)
items.back: 1. When identification is established by position correlation. 2. When a departing aircraft is identified within 1 mile of the takeoff runway end.

items.front: Two times to advise radar contact (5-3-7a)
items.back: 1. When initial radar identification is established. 2. When radar identification is reestablished after loss of radar contact or termination of radar service.

items.front: Radar contact phraseology (5-3-7a)
items.back: RADAR CONTACT (position if required).

items.front: Radar contact lost phraseology (5-3-7b)
items.back: RADAR CONTACT LOST (alternative instructions when required).

items.front: En route data block retention rule (5-3-8)
items.back: Retain data blocks associated with appropriate target symbol until aircraft has exited the sector or delegated airspace AND all potential conflicts resolved, including point-outs. Minimum display: flight ID and altitude.

items.front: Acceptable altitude types for en route data block (5-3-8)
items.back: Assigned, interim, or reported.

items.front: ERAM full data block requirement (5-3-8)
items.back: When you have separation responsibility for an aircraft and a paired track exists, display a full data block (FDB).

items.front: Terminal data block retention rule (5-3-9a)
items.back: Same as en route: retain until aircraft has exited sector/delegated airspace and all potential conflicts resolved, including point-outs. Minimum display: flight ID and altitude.

items.front: VFR data block retention exception in terminal (5-3-9a note)
items.back: Where delegated airspace extends beyond Class B/C: if VFR aircraft is clear of Class B and Class C airspace AND radar services terminated, retention no longer required.

items.front: Prearranged coordination data block requirement (5-3-9b)
items.back: Controller penetrating another controller's airspace must display that controller's aircraft data block. Minimum: position symbol and altitude information.
